geopolitics_summary: Strait of Hormuz closure since late February pressuring global LNG prices; EU gas imports face elevated risk.

themes.headline: International LNG prices rise amid Strait of Hormuz closure
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz blockade disrupts Middle East LNG exports to EU; TTF spreads vs. Asian LNG widen, tightening EU supply and raising gas cost of power. Direct transmission to DA power prices via fuel-mix switching.

themes.headline: DOE releases 17.5M barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve since March
themes.why_it_matters: SPR releases suppress Brent; lower oil indirectly eases EU gas prices via LNG arbitrage feedback (weaker oil anchor reduces LNG cost to Europe). Modest downward pressure on power via fuel-cost relief.

themes.headline: US renewable diesel and SAF exports reach 50k b/d in 2H25
themes.why_it_matters: US biofuel export surge diverts feedstock from potential EU renewable fuel blending, indirectly pressuring EU decarbonisation cost curves. Limited direct power impact; marginal carbon policy signal.

watchlist: Strait of Hormuz status updates; assess LNG supply route restoration timeline.
SPR release schedule completion; rebound in Brent could shift LNG arb and TTF.
EU LNG contract renegotiations and spot import flows; monitor 2W forward curve.
